_variables.brand_name: Display name of the brand (e.g. TONAL)
_variables.brand_slug: Lowercase slug used in directories and URLs (e.g. tonal)
_variables.product_category: Primary product category (e.g. clothing, accessories, home goods, beauty)
_variables.aesthetic_refs: Comma-separated aesthetic references (e.g. COS, Aesop, Everlane)
_variables.fulfillment_provider: Print-on-demand or fulfillment partner (e.g. TapStitch, Printful, custom warehouse)
_variables.domain: Production domain for the storefront (e.g. weartonal.com)
_variables.github_org: GitHub organization or user for the repo (e.g. {your-name})
_variables.product_types: Comma-separated product types (e.g. tees, hoodies, sweaters, bombers)
_variables.pricing_position: Pricing strategy description (e.g. premium basics, 60-70% margin over fulfillment cost)

name: {{brand_slug}}
description: Launch {{brand_name}} — a DTC {{product_category}} brand on headless Shopify (Next.js + Storefront API) with {{fulfillment_provider}} fulfillment

userStories.title: Brand Identity
userStories.description: As a founder, I want a cohesive brand identity for {{brand_name}} so that the store has a professional, memorable presence
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette defined: primary ({{color_primary}}), secondary ({{color_secondary}}), accent ({{color_accent}}), neutrals — inspired by {{aesthetic_refs}}
Typography selected: heading ({{font_heading}}) + body ({{font_body}}) from Google Fonts or similar
Simple wordmark/logo created (SVG + PNG formats)
Brand guidelines document written and committed to companies/{{brand_slug}}/knowledge/brand-guidelines.md
Domain {{domain}} confirmed available and purchase instructions documented
userStories.notes: Use frontend-designer worker. Aesthetic direction: {{aesthetic_refs}}. {{brand_name}} = {{brand_description}}. For non-clothing brands: replace 'wordmark' with appropriate logo type (icon, monogram, emblem) based on product category.

userStories.title: Product Strategy + Catalog JSON
userStories.description: As a store owner, I want a product catalog defined so that all items, pricing, and variants are documented before Shopify setup
acceptanceCriteria: Product catalog JSON created at companies/{{brand_slug}}/data/product-catalog.json
Catalog follows playbook schema: metadata, collections, products array
All product types covered: {{product_types}}
Pricing set with {{pricing_position}} margins (target 60-70% gross margin for POD)
Size options configured: {{size_range}} per product type
Collections defined with handles, titles, descriptions, sort orders
Product descriptions written in {{brand_name}} brand voice
SKU format documented: {handle}-{color-slug}-{size}
userStories.notes: Single source-of-truth catalog file drives both Shopify product push (US-003) and storefront display. See playbook section 3 (Catalog Pipeline) for schema. For non-clothing brands: adapt variant matrix (e.g. scent+size for candles, material+finish for home goods) — the 2-option variant pattern (Color x Size) is clothing-specific. Inventory policy: 'continue' for print-on-demand (no stock tracking).

userStories.title: Shopify Setup + Product Push
userStories.description: As a store owner, I want a Shopify store configured with all products pushed so that the headless backend is ready
acceptanceCriteria: Shopify store created at {{shopify_store_slug}}.myshopify.com (Basic plan, $39/mo)
{{fulfillment_provider}} app installed and connected
Custom app created in Shopify Dev Dashboard with required API scopes
Credentials stored in companies/{{brand_slug}}/settings/shopify/.env (client_id, client_secret/shpss_, shpat_, storefront token)
Product push script created at companies/{{brand_slug}}/data/setup-products.ts
All products from catalog JSON pushed to Shopify via Admin REST API
Collections created and products linked via Collects API
All products verified active in Shopify admin
userStories.notes: MANUAL PREREQUISITE: User must create the Shopify store and Dev Dashboard app before this story executes. See playbook section 1 (Auth Flow) for 2026 auth model: client_credentials grant, 24h token expiry, shpss_ dual-purpose token. Required scopes: write_products, read_products, write_inventory, read_inventory, read_product_images, write_product_images, read_orders. Rate limiting: 500ms between collections, 1000ms between products. Use --dry-run flag for testing.

userStories.title: Product Imagery
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want clean product images so that I can see what I am buying before purchase
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 images per product (front view + detail/lifestyle shot)
Consistent styling: white/light background, flat lay or on-model, matching {{aesthetic_refs}} aesthetic
Source PNGs generated via AI (Gemini Imagen 3) or provided by user
WebP optimization script created at companies/{{brand_slug}}/data/optimize-images.sh
Images converted to 3 responsive sizes: 400w, 800w, 1200w WebP
All product images uploaded to Shopify CDN via Admin API
Hero/lifestyle images created for homepage (16:9 aspect ratio)
Variant-image associations set for color-specific shots
userStories.notes: See playbook section 4 (Imagery Pipeline) for WebP optimization script and directory structure. For non-clothing brands: adapt image types (e.g. product-in-use shots for home goods, ingredient/texture shots for beauty, detail/packaging shots for accessories). AI generation via gnb CLI (Gemini 3 Pro) works well for flat-lay product shots. Admin API token must be regenerated (24h expiry) before upload session.

userStories.title: Scaffold Storefront
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the storefront repo initialized from a template so that we can build the store quickly
acceptanceCriteria: Repo created at repos/private/{{brand_slug}}-store from Vercel Commerce template
pnpm install succeeds with no errors
TypeScript + Tailwind CSS configured
Shopify Storefront API connected via .env.local
Products load from Shopify and render on dev server
GitHub repo created (private) under {{github_org}}
Repo added to companies/{{brand_slug}} manifest entry
qmd collection created for {{brand_slug}}-store
userStories.notes: Scaffold from Vercel Commerce template (https://vercel.com/templates/next.js/nextjs-commerce). Provides: product grid, PDP, cart, Shopify integration, Tailwind, TypeScript. The Storefront API token is the shpss_ token (same as client_secret). See playbook section 5 (Storefront Architecture) for file structure reference.

userStories.title: Brand Design + All Pages
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want the store to reflect the {{brand_name}} brand identity with all content pages so that it feels cohesive and premium
acceptanceCriteria: Brand colors applied to Tailwind theme config (primary: {{color_primary}}, secondary: {{color_secondary}}, accent: {{color_accent}})
Typography loaded and applied: {{font_heading}} (headings) + {{font_body}} (body)
Logo/wordmark placed in header and footer
Homepage customized: hero section, featured products grid, brand story snippet, lifestyle imagery
About page created with brand story, values, and mission
Consistent component styling (buttons, cards, navigation) matches brand guidelines
Mobile-first responsive design verified on 3 breakpoints (375px, 768px, 1280px)
Navigation links to all pages in header and footer
userStories.notes: Merges brand design customization + about/brand story page into one story (was 2 stories in TONAL). Use frontend-designer worker for creative direction, frontend-dev for implementation. Brand guidelines from companies/{{brand_slug}}/knowledge/brand-guidelines.md drive all design decisions. For non-clothing brands: adapt homepage sections (e.g. 'How It Works' for beauty, 'Materials' for home goods, 'Ingredients' for food).

userStories.title: Product Pages (PLP + PDP)
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want to browse and view products so that I can decide what to buy
acceptanceCriteria: Product listing page (PLP) with grid layout showing all products
Filter by collection on PLP
Color swatches shown on PLP product cards
Product detail page (PDP) with image gallery
Color and size selector on PDP
Add to cart button on PDP
Size chart/guide accessible from PDP
SEO meta tags and Product JSON-LD structured data on PDP
Loading skeletons and error boundaries for API calls
userStories.notes: PLP and PDP are the core shopping experience. Use Server Components for SEO (product pages need full SSR with meta tags and JSON-LD). Client components for interactivity (color/size selectors, add-to-cart). For non-clothing brands: adapt variant selectors (e.g. scent picker for candles, material selector for furniture) and size guide (e.g. dimensions guide for home goods, usage guide for beauty).

userStories.title: Cart + Checkout
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want to add items to cart and checkout so that I can purchase products
acceptanceCriteria: Slide-out cart or dedicated cart page implemented
Cart shows line items with thumbnail, name, variant details, quantity, price
Update quantity and remove items from cart
Cart total updates in real-time
Checkout button redirects to Shopify hosted checkout via cart permalink
Cart state persists across page navigation via localStorage + React context
Cart icon in header shows item count badge
Graceful error handling for checkout redirect failures
userStories.notes: Use cart permalink pattern (NOT Draft Order API) — see playbook section 2 (Checkout Strategy) for rationale. Cart permalink: https://{{shopify_store_slug}}.myshopify.com/cart/{variant_id}:{quantity}. localStorage-backed React context for cart state (avoids Storefront API cart token dependency). For non-clothing brands: same cart pattern applies regardless of product type.

userStories.title: Deploy + SEO
userStories.description: As a store owner, I want the store live on {{domain}} with SEO configured so that customers can find and access it
acceptanceCriteria: Vercel project created under {{vercel_org}}
GitHub repo connected for auto-deploy on push to main
All environment variables set in Vercel production
Custom domain {{domain}} connected with DNS configured and SSL active
Framework set to nextjs (NOT null — see playbook section 7 for 404 gotcha)
sitemap.xml auto-generated via Next.js app/sitemap.ts
robots.txt configured via app/robots.ts
OpenGraph and Twitter/X card meta tags on all pages
Canonical URLs set on all pages
Favicon and app icons generated from brand identity
Page titles follow pattern: {Page} | {{brand_name}}
Production build succeeds with no errors and all routes return 200
userStories.notes: Merges deployment + SEO/analytics into one story (was 2 in TONAL). See playbook section 7 (Vercel Deployment) for framework:null gotcha and domain team move issue. See playbook section 8 (SEO Checklist) for full SEO requirements. MANUAL PREREQUISITE: domain must be purchased before DNS configuration. Vercel org mapping: check companies/manifest.yaml for correct scope.

userStories.title: Launch QA
userStories.description: As a store owner, I want everything tested before launch so that customers have a smooth experience
acceptanceCriteria: Full purchase flow tested: browse > PLP > PDP > add to cart > checkout redirect works
Mobile responsiveness verified on 3 breakpoints (375px, 768px, 1280px)
Cross-browser check: Chrome, Safari, Firefox
Lighthouse audit: Performance > 90, Accessibility > 90, SEO = 100, Best Practices = 100
No console errors in production build
Shopify store password protection disabled
Test order placed through full checkout flow and confirmed in Shopify admin
{{fulfillment_provider}} receives test order and fulfillment flow confirmed
Shipping notification email fires when fulfilled
Custom domain resolves with valid SSL
All critical issues from QA fixed before go-live
userStories.notes: See playbook section 9 (QA Checklist) for comprehensive checklist. Use qa-tester worker. MANUAL PREREQUISITES: Shopify store password protection must be disabled in Settings > Online Store > Preferences before test orders can be placed. For non-clothing brands: adapt QA scenarios to product type (e.g. verify subscription flow for consumables, verify customization options for personalized products).

metadata.goal: Launch {{brand_name}} as a DTC {{product_category}} brand on {{domain}} with headless Shopify + {{fulfillment_provider}} fulfillment
metadata.successCriteria: Store live at {{domain}} with full product catalog, working checkout, and at least one test order fulfilled via {{fulfillment_provider}}
